task_completion.description: Create a Spreadsheet object and define the required parameters with aliases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Spreadsheet view is open in FreeCAD showing labels for 'base_length', 'base_width', 'upright_height', and 'thickness' with corresponding numeric values in adjacent cells. Aliased cells often have a yellow background.

task_completion.description: Create an L-profile sketch with dimensions linked to the spreadsheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sketcher workbench is visible showing an L-shaped profile. Dimension constraints are present, and ideally show formula indicators (like a small 'f(x)' icon or orange color) indicating they are driven by expressions.

task_completion.description: Pad the sketch to create the 3D L-bracket geometry.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D solid L-bracket is visible in the 3D view (typically in the Part Design workbench). The Model tree should show a 'Pad' feature.

task_completion.description: Update the upright_height parameter from 50 mm to 70 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet shows the value '70' next to 'upright_height', and/or the 3D model of the L-bracket visibly updates to have a taller upright section.
The FreeCAD window title bar or the root node in the Model tree view displays the filename 'parametric_bracket' or 'parametric_bracket.FCStd'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the FreeCAD graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeCAD application window with its toolbars, Model/Tasks panels, and 3D view, rather than just a terminal window executing a script.

integrity.description: The agent used FreeCAD's internal Spreadsheet workbench.
integrity.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet is viewed within the FreeCAD application tab/window, not in an external application like LibreOffice Calc or Microsoft Excel.
The 3D view shows a rendered solid model with shading and edges, and the Model tree shows valid FreeCAD features (e.g., Sketch, Pad), not an imported image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The final expected parameters in the spreadsheet are: base_length=60, base_width=40, upright_height=70 (updated from 50), and thickness=5. The final bounding box of the 3D geometry should be approximately 60x40x70 mm. The expected filename is 'parametric_bracket.FCStd'.
